code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject so we can build one sentence per subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Predicate → clause template that already contains the subject where appropriate
    clause_templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has runway {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "the ICAO identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "the location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "the IATA identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the first runway is {obj} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the first runway is {obj} metres long",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "the first runway surface is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "the runway surface is {obj}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "the fourth runway surface is {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "the fifth runway surface is {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "the fifth runway number is {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "the runway length of {subject} is {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "the leader of {subject} is {obj}",
        "capital": "the capital of {subject} is {obj}",
        "language": "the language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "the official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "currency": "the currency of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "the owner of {subject} is {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "{subject} was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "the postal code of {subject} is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "the area code of {subject} is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "the largest city in {subject} is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "the county seat of {subject} is {obj}",
        "mayor": "the mayor of {subject} is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "the leader party of {subject} is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title of {subject} is {obj}",
        "chief": "the chief of {subject} is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "the hub airport of {subject} is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves the region of {obj}",
        "nativeName": "the native name of {subject} is {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "the class of {subject} is {obj}",
        "division": "the division of {subject} is {obj}",
        "order": "the order of {subject} is {obj}",
        "species": "the species of {subject} is {obj}",
        "demonym": "the demonym for {subject} is {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        clauses = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = clause_templates.get(t.predicate)
            obj = t.object.strip('"')
            if tmpl:
                # Some templates already include the subject placeholder
                clause = tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj)
            else:
                # Fallback: simple “{predicate} {object}” phrasing
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                clause = f"{pred} {obj}"
            clauses.append(clause)

        # Build a single fluent sentence per subject
        if not clauses:
            continue
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = f"{clauses[0].capitalize()}."
        else:
            # Join with commas, add “and” before the last clause
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
            sentence = f"{subject} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
